label: ProxLab: Scheduled Invocation
info:
Create a scheduled agent invocation that runs on an interval or at a specific time of day.
The schedule persists in HA and survives restarts.

rules.to: {"agent_id": "security", "schedule_type": "interval", "schedule_config": {"seconds": 1800}, "message_template": "Perform a routine security check of all systems.", "cooldown_seconds": 60}

{"agent_id": "reporting", "schedule_type": "time_of_day", "schedule_config": {"hour": 8, "minute": 0, "second": 0}, "message_template": "Generate a morning summary report of all systems, energy usage, and notable events from overnight."}

name: How to use
info:
Two example schedules:

1. INTERVAL (top): Runs the security agent every 30 minutes
   - schedule_type: interval
   - schedule_config.seconds: 1800 (30 min)

2. TIME OF DAY (bottom): Runs the reporting agent daily at 8:00 AM
   - schedule_type: time_of_day
   - schedule_config: {hour: 8, minute: 0, second: 0}

Click the inject button to create the schedule. It persists in HA.
Manage schedules from the ProxLab panel Schedules page.

Available agents: general, repairman, security, reporting, worker, etc.
